- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www/html
    depends_on:
      mysql:
        condition: service_healthy
    environment:
      APP_ENV: local
      PHP_OPCACHE_ENABLE: 0

  nginx:
    image: nginx:1.25-alpine
    ports:
      - "${APP_PORT:-8085}:80"
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www/html
      - ./docker/nginx/default.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    depends_on:
      - app

  mysql:
    image: mysql:8.0
    environment:
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: rootsecret
      MYSQL_DATABASE: ${DB_DATABASE:-vcard_app}
      MYSQL_USER: ${DB_USERNAME:-vcard}
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-secret}
    ports:
      - "${DB_PORT:-3306}:3306"
    volumes:
      - mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "mysqladmin", "ping", "-h", "localhost", "-u", "root", "-prootsecret"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 12

  node:
    image: node:20-alpine
    working_dir: /var/www/html
    volumes:
      - .:/var/www/html
    ports:
      - "${VITE_PORT:-5173}:5173"
    command: sh -c "npm install && npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0"
    profiles:
      - dev

volumes:
  mysql_data:

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

echo "=== VCard Manager — First Time Setup ==="

# Copy env
if [ ! -f .env ]; then
  cp .env.example .env
  echo "✓ .env created from .env.example"
fi

# Start MySQL
echo "Starting MySQL..."
docker compose up -d mysql
echo "Waiting for MySQL to be ready (up to 60s)..."
timeout=60
until docker compose exec mysql mysqladmin ping -h localhost -u root -prootsecret --silent 2>/dev/null || [ $timeout -le 0 ]; do
  sleep 2
  timeout=$((timeout - 2))
done

# Scaffold Laravel (skip files that already exist)
echo "Creating Laravel scaffold (this may take a minute)..."
docker compose run --rm --no-deps app sh -c "
  cd /tmp && composer create-project laravel/laravel scaffold --prefer-dist --no-scripts -q 2>/dev/null
  for item in /tmp/scaffold/.[!.]* /tmp/scaffold/*; do
    [ -e \"\$item\" ] || continue
    name=\$(basename \"\$item\")
    [ -e \"/var/www/html/\$name\" ] || cp -r \"\$item\" \"/var/www/html/\$name\"
  done
  echo '✓ Laravel scaffold done'
"

# Install Composer deps
echo "Installing PHP dependencies..."
docker compose run --rm --no-deps app composer install --no-interaction

# Generate app key
docker compose run --rm --no-deps app php artisan key:generate

# Fix storage permissions
docker compose run --rm --no-deps app sh -c "
  mkdir -p storage/app/public/avatars storage/app/public/logos storage/app/public/portfolio
  chmod -R 777 storage bootstrap/cache
"

# Migrate and seed
echo "Running migrations and seeds..."
docker compose run --rm app php artisan migrate --seed --force

# Storage symlink
docker compose run --rm app php artisan storage:link 2>/dev/null || true

# Node deps + Tailwind
echo "Installing Node dependencies..."
docker compose run --rm --no-deps -e HOME=/tmp node sh -c "
  npm install &&
  npm install alpinejs &&
  npm install -D tailwindcss @tailwindcss/forms autoprefixer
"

# Build assets (creates public/build for production-style serving)
echo "Building frontend assets..."
docker compose run --rm --no-deps -e HOME=/tmp node sh -c "npm run build"

echo ""
echo "=== Setup complete! ==="
echo ""
echo "  Production-style:  docker compose up"
echo "  With HMR (dev):    docker compose --profile dev up"
echo "  Open:              http://localhost:8085"
echo ""
echo "  Accounts:"
echo "    super@vcardapp.com   / password  (super admin)"
echo "    admin1@vcardapp.com  / password  (5 seats)"
echo "    admin2@vcardapp.com  / password  (3 seats)"
echo "    admin3@vcardapp.com  / password  (3 seats)"

Be very specific about colors (hex if visible), spacing, border-radius, shadows, and overall aesthetic. content.content:
# Ryan Adlard vCard Template - Design Analysis

## Overall Layout & Structure
This is a single-page portfolio template featuring a card-based design with multiple stacked sections. The layout appears to use a centered, vertical scrolling structure with distinct content cards for different areas.

## Color Scheme
The document doesn't explicitly specify hex codes, but the design suggests:
- **Primary background**: Light/white (modern, clean aesthetic)
- **Text**: Dark gray/black for readability
- **Accents**: Likely blues or professional tones (typical for web designer portfolios)
- **Call-to-action buttons**: Distinct color differentiation for "Buy Basic," "Buy Pro," and "Contact Me"

## Typography
- **Heading hierarchy**: Multiple heading levels for section organization
- **Body text**: Standard serif/sans-serif pairing (not specified)
- **Navigation links**: Anchor-based (#about-card, #resume-card, etc.) suggesting smooth scrolling

## Navigation & Sections
**Main Navigation Tabs:**
- About
- Resume
- Works
- Blog
- Contact

**Key Content Sections:**
1. **Hero Profile Area**: Name, title ("Web Designer," "Developer," "Freelancer"), social links
2. **About Card**: Bio, personal details, services overview
3. **Services**: Four categories (Web Development, Music Writing, Advertising, Game Development)
4. **Pricing Cards**: Tiered packages (Basic $22/hr, Pro $48/hr)
5. **Resume**: Experience timeline, education, skills with proficiency percentages
6. **Portfolio**: Categorized works (Image, Gallery, Video, Music, Content) with modal popups
7. **Blog**: Recent posts with pagination
8. **Contact**: Form and information section
9. **Sidebar**: Recent posts, comments, archives, categories

## Card & Component Styles
- **Service cards**: Feature-based layout describing offerings
- **Pricing cards**: Side-by-side comparison with bullet points and CTAs
- **Portfolio cards**: Hover-enabled with category labels
- **Testimonial cards**: User photo, name, title, quote
- **Blog cards**: Image, date, title, excerpt with read-more links

## Visual Elements
- **Profile image**: Circular/rounded avatar (images/profile.png)
- **Client logos**: Linked image gallery
- **Work thumbnails**: Categorized project previews
- **Social icons**: Dribbble, Twitter, GitHub, Spotify, Stack Overflow placeholders
- **Skill bars**: WordPress (90%), PHP/MySQL (75%), Angular/JavaScript (85%), HTML/CSS (95%)
- **Fun facts section**: Statistics display (80 Albums, 15 Awards, 1000 Cups, 10 Countries)

## Interactive Elements
- **Buttons**: "Download CV," "Contact Me," "Buy Basic/Pro," "View Project," "Send Message"
- **Links**: Modal popups for project details (#popup-1, #popup-2)
- **Gallery features**: Lightbox/popup functionality for works
- **Form**: Contact submission with success message

## Aesthetic & Tone
Modern, minimalist professional portfolio emphasizing creative capabilities across web design, development, music, and advertising. The design balances visual hierarchy with whitespace, suggesting contemporary freelance/agency branding standards.

Done. Here's exactly what changed and why:

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| Cards stretched full viewport height | `body` is now `display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; padding:28px` — everything is vertically centered with green showing above/below |
| `.page` filled `min-height:100vh` | Replaced with `max-width:1260px; align-items:flex-start; gap:12px` — natural content height only |
| Icon nav was `height:100vh; position:sticky` | Now `align-self:flex-start; border-radius:8px; position:sticky; top:28px` — a proper floating card |
| Profile card had outer margins fighting the layout | Margins removed; gap between items handled by `.page { gap:12px }` |
| Content card had no scroll cap | Now `max-height:calc(100vh - 56px); overflow-y:auto` — scrolls internally like Ryan |

Refresh **http://localhost:8085/dominos/daniel-marquez** — the three cards should now float centered on the green gradient exactly like Ryan's design.
